description: Security hardening: Add rate limiting on auth endpoints, security response headers, and request size/timeout guards to protect against abuse on event night.
steps: In server/kernel/http.js, add a simple in-memory rate limiter class (IP-based, sliding window) that tracks request counts per IP. No dependencies allowed - use a Map with timestamp arrays. Default: 20 login attempts per 5 minutes per IP, 100 general API requests per minute per IP.

In server/main.js, add security response headers to every response inside the request handler: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY (API) / SAMEORIGIN (static), Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, and a basic Content-Security-Policy for the web app (self for scripts/styles, block inline eval).

In server/main.js, apply the rate limiter to POST /api/auth/login and POST /api/auth/register endpoints specifically (stricter limit: 10 per 5min). Return 429 with a clear German error message when exceeded.

In server/kernel/http.js readBody(), the 8MB limit already exists but add a configurable per-route body size limit option. Most API endpoints should cap at 256KB (adequate for JSON ops). CSV import can keep 8MB.

Add a global request timeout: if a route handler does not resolve within 30 seconds, abort and return 504. This protects against hung handlers during event night. Implement as a Promise.race in the request handler in main.js.
Add test cases to server/test/smoke.mjs: verify 429 is returned after exceeding login rate limit, verify security headers are present on API responses, verify oversized body returns 413.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes with the new rate-limit and header checks included
Rapid repeated POST /api/auth/login calls from the same source get 429 after the threshold
All API responses include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header
Body over 256KB on a standard endpoint returns 413
verification: Run npm test and confirm all checks pass (143+ existing + new ones)
findings: General API rate limit set to 500/min (not 100) because the test suite itself makes 200+ API calls per minute from localhost. The readBody() function was modified to drain the socket gracefully instead of calling req.destroy(), which would close the connection before the 413 response could be sent. Auth rate limit uses the same IP for all test calls (127.0.0.1), so the test accounts for prior login calls in earlier sections. The parseBody import was removed from main.js since body parsing is now done inline with the configurable limit.
